name: Fine Swiss Weisswurst
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the veal with salt and sugar.
2. Grind the meat and jowl fat through a fine plate of a heavy-duty grinder.
3. Keep the meat and jowl fat separate and chill well.
4. Place the ground meat in a food processor and add ice.
5. Sprinkle the spice mixture over the ice and process until very cold (30°F on a thermometer).
6. Stop the machine, scrape down the sides.
7. Continue processing until the temperature rises to 40°F (the longer it takes, the finer the sausage).
8. Add jowl fat and process until the mixture reaches 45°F (emulsification occurs).
9. Add non-fat milk powder and process until the mixture reaches 58°F.
To stuff casings:
1. Fill a commercial sausage stuffer evenly with the mixture, ensuring there are no air pockets.
2. Stuff hog casings loosely, tying off with fine kitchen string.
3. Poach sausages in a shallow pan of simmering water (water should be 165°F), until the internal temperature of the meat reaches 155°F.
4. Remove sausages to an ice bath when cooked. When the internal temperature reaches 60°F, remove from water.
5. Cook, fry, or grill and serve.
